heading: Enhanced supervision and prudential standards for nonbank financial companies supervised by the Board of Governors and certain bank holding companies
label: 5325 Enhanced supervision and prudential standards for nonbank financial companies supervised by the Board of Governors and certain bank holding companies

children.label: A
children.text: differentiate among companies that are subject to heightened standards on an individual basis or by category, taking into consideration their capital structure, riskiness, complexity, financial activities (including the financial activities of their subsidiaries), size, and any other risk-related factors that the Council deems appropriate; or

children.heading: Prudential standards for foreign financial companies
children.label: 2 Prudential standards for foreign financial companies
A
children.text: give due regard to the principle of national treatment and equality of competitive opportunity; and

children.label: B
children.text: take into account the extent to which the foreign nonbank financial company or foreign-based bank holding company is subject on a consolidated basis to home country standards that are comparable to those applied to financial companies in the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: the factors described in subsections (a) and (b) of ;section 5323 of this title

children.label: ii

children.label: iii

children.label: iv

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent possible, ensure that small changes in the factors listed in subsections (a) and (b) of would not result in sharp, discontinuous changes in the prudential standards established under ; andsection 5323 of this titlesection 5365 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: adapt its recommendations as appropriate in light of any predominant line of business of such company, including assets under management or other activities for which particular standards may not be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: an evaluation of the degree to which such requirement would enhance the safety and soundness of companies subject to the requirement, promote the financial stability of the United States, and reduce risks to United States taxpayers;

children.label: B
children.text: an evaluation of the characteristics and amounts of contingent capital that should be required;

children.label: C
children.text: an analysis of potential prudential standards that should be used to determine whether the contingent capital of a company would be converted to equity in times of financial stress;

children.label: D
children.text: an evaluation of the costs to companies, the effects on the structure and operation of credit and other financial markets, and other economic effects of requiring contingent capital;

children.label: E
children.text: an evaluation of the effects of such requirement on the international competitiveness of companies subject to the requirement and the prospects for international coordination in establishing such requirement; and

children.label: F
children.text: recommendations for implementing regulations.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: The Council shall submit a report to Congress regarding the study required by paragraph (1) not later than 2 years after .July 21, 2010

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subsequent to submitting a report to Congress under paragraph (2), the Council may make recommendations to the Board of Governors to require any nonbank financial company supervised by the Board of Governors and any bank holding company described in subsection (a) to maintain a minimum amount of contingent capital that is convertible to equity in times of financial stress.

children.label: i
children.text: an appropriate transition period for implementation of a conversion under this subsection;

children.label: ii
children.text: the factors described in subsection (b)(3);

children.label: iii
children.text: capital requirements applicable to a nonbank financial company supervised by the Board of Governors or a bank holding company described in subsection (a), and subsidiaries thereof;

children.label: iv
children.text: results of the study required by paragraph (1); and

children.label: v
children.text: any other factor that the Council deems appropriate.

children.heading: Resolution plan and credit exposure reports
children.label: d Resolution plan and credit exposure reports

children.heading: Resolution plan
children.label: 1 Resolution plan
children.text: The Council may make recommendations to the Board of Governors concerning the requirement that each nonbank financial company supervised by the Board of Governors and each bank holding company described in subsection (a) report periodically to the Council, the Board of Governors, and the Corporation, the plan of such company for rapid and orderly resolution in the event of material financial distress or failure.

children.label: A
children.text: the nature and extent to which the company has credit exposure to other significant nonbank financial companies and significant bank holding companies; and

children.label: B
children.text: the nature and extent to which other such significant nonbank financial companies and significant bank holding companies have credit exposure to that company.

children.heading: Concentration limits
children.label: e Concentration limits
children.text: In order to limit the risks that the failure of any individual company could pose to nonbank financial companies supervised by the Board of Governors or bank holding companies described in subsection (a), the Council may make recommendations to the Board of Governors to prescribe standards to limit such risks, as set forth in .section 5365 of this title

children.heading: Enhanced public disclosures
children.label: f Enhanced public disclosures
children.text: The Council may make recommendations to the Board of Governors to require periodic public disclosures by bank holding companies described in subsection (a) and by nonbank financial companies supervised by the Board of Governors, in order to support market evaluation of the risk profile, capital adequacy, and risk management capabilities thereof.

children.heading: Short-term debt limits
children.label: g Short-term debt limits
children.text: The Council may make recommendations to the Board of Governors to require short-term debt limits to mitigate the risks that an over-accumulation of such debt could pose to bank holding companies described in subsection (a), nonbank financial companies supervised by the Board of Governors, or the financial system.
